title: I Tested the ARC Pulse for the iPhone 17 Pro Max!📱
transcript: Okay, so I tried the Ark Pulse and here's my mini review. It's a bumper style case made for people who don't like using cases. It comes in two pieces which are made from aluminum or titanium and the inside has rubber inserts for shock absorption. All you do is slide it on and boom. I like how I can still see and feel the foam while having drop protection and no more rocking on a table. There's also a generous ledge around the camera plateau for my index finger to rest on, and the phone can easily stand upright in portrait or landscape. The Ark Pulse is on the pricey side, starting at $70, and taller MagSafe accessories won't sit flush. It's very minimal, looks premium, and I'll be keeping it on my

title: The iPhone 17 is the BEST!!
transcript: So, as we get closer to the holiday season, I'm just going to come on out and say it. The best phone for 2025 is the regular iPhone 17. >> I know, I know. Feel free to leave your Apple fanboy jabs in the comments, but I think I have a good argument to support this claim. Number one, this is by far the most feature-p packed non-pro iPhone Apple has ever released. You get an updated display with a much overdue 120 Hz variable refresh rate and a blazing fast new mobile processor. Now, yes, it's true competitors have had these features for a long time now. But there are some unique features that the iPhone 17 brings to the table. For example, you get Apple's new center stage front-facing selfie camera. That's really awesome. This totally enhances the utility of the camera that we use a lot more than we think, and it's a really smart upgrade. Moreover, the iPhone 17 gives you some of the best video quality that you can get on a phone, period. The 4K video that you're able to get with this phone trumps the non iPhone competition, and it's super important if you like to take and share videos. And last, this phone is really well futurep proof to last you a long time. So you'll be able to take advantage of all the new features Apple has planned for the future so you don't miss out. What phone do you think is the best for 2025?

title: I Tried Apple’s New Clear Case on the iPhone 17 Pro – Here’s the Truth
transcript: Apple's clear case design has changed this year and they've introduced this white rectangle that covers most of the back instead of the previous white ring for the Mag Safe. The white ring is still visible on the inside, but you can't see it from the outside anymore. When Apple first showed this design of the keynote, I didn't like it at all. I couldn't see the point in having a white rectangle on a clear case. But when you look at the back of the silver iPhone 17 Pro, there's actually already a white rectangle in place, which is where the ceramic shield back is. So really, the clear case just follows the same design and actually looks okay. And I think when you put this case on it, it actually looks really nice. It kind of blends in with the silver iPhone. I was convinced I wouldn't like it on the orange or blue versions, but I recently got a chance to try it on the orange iPhone, and I actually really liked it. I know it's not technically a clear case, but I like the two-tone version. It looks pretty good with the orange. I would imagine it would look pretty good on the blue as well, but I haven't tried this, so it's hard to say for sure. Unlike the tech woven and silicon case, the logo matches the same position as on the back of the iPhone. At the bottom of the phone, there was a large gap that spans the charger and speakers, so you do have this area exposed. I've had some comments that people find a lot of dirt gets in and around this area, and because it's clear, it can look a bit dirty over time. I'm not sure having this big gap is good or bad, but it's nice having it all protected like the other cases for the extra peace of mind, even if it's just for a few scuffs and scratches. There are no holes for the lanyard straps either on this case, so if that's what you want, this is not the case for you. The case is quite rigid and doesn't flex too much. And I was asked on my unboxing video what the edges of the case felt like, and I'd say they are pretty smooth with no hard edges. It's quite comfortable to hold. On the sides, everything is clear except for the camera control button, which is white. Similar to the tech woven and silicon cases, all the buttons feel nice and clicky. And due to the massive camera bump that spans the length of the phone, you don't get any wobbles if you're typing on a table. When you pair it with the iPhone 17 Pro in silver and orange, this is what it looks like. So, overall, I think it feels really comfortable to hold and it looks really smart. It complements the silver really well. And I do like it on the orange for that two-tone look. I'm sure there'll be plenty of comments below saying how ugly it is, though. Overall, for my first impressions, I really like it. It'll be interesting to see how it holds up over the long term and if it starts to turn yellow or
